query: Terrion Arnold Detroit Lions Commissioner's Exempt List released waived NFL transaction status July 2026
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's the bottom line on Terrion Arnold's transaction status as of July 2026:

**Current status: WAIVED → CLEARED WAIVERS → FREE AGENT (NOT on Commissioner's Exempt List)**

- The Detroit Lions **waived** him on **June 29, 2026** (technically "waived," not released outright, since he had fewer than four accrued seasons), hours after a Florida judge granted his release on a $1 million bond.
- He **cleared waivers unclaimed on Monday, July 6, 2026**, becoming a free agent.
- As of the latest reporting (**July 10, 2026**), he had **NOT** been placed on the Commissioner's Exempt List. The Lions specifically chose to part ways rather than use the exempt list. That list only becomes a live possibility *if a team signs him*, at which point the NFL could place him on it.
- He faces **eight felony charges** in Florida (kidnapping/armed robbery allegations from February 2026) and remains unsigned, with four teams (Texans, Jets, Colts, Seahawks) reportedly interested.

Note the potential terminology confusion: the exempt list was never used for Arnold — the Lions simply cut him, he cleared waivers, and he's now an unsigned free agent.

sources.title: Lions Waive CB Terrion Arnold
sources.snippet: The Lions waived cornerback Terrion Arnold per a team announcement on June 29, 2026, hours after a judge granted his release on $1 million bond. The team did not place him on the Commissioner's Exempt List.

sources.title: Former Lions CB Terrion Arnold clears waivers, 3 teams interested
sources.snippet: On Monday, July 6, 2026, former Detroit Lions cornerback Terrion Arnold cleared waivers, becoming a free agent. Commissioner's Exempt List noted as a possibility but not current status.

sources.title: Former Lions CB Terrion Arnold draws interest from 4 NFL teams
sources.snippet: July 10, 2026: Arnold cleared waivers and is a free agent; interested teams are Texans, Jets, Colts, Seahawks. Not on Commissioner's Exempt List, but NFL reserves the right to place him there if signed.
